title: See Autonomous Chip Design in Action with ChipAgents at DAC 2026 - Semiwiki
gist: ChipAgents is showcasing production-grade agentic AI for chip design at DAC 2026, claiming 3x higher pass-at-one accuracy than generic AI agents on root-cause analysis and, in a joint paper with STMicroelectronics, verification speedups of 15x (spec reading), 240x (formal assertion generation) and 400x (UVM environment creation) under full engineer control.

themes.theme: Agentic AI moving into production EDA

insights.claim: For chip design and verification teams the debate has shifted from whether AI can assist engineers to where AI delivers measurable value today, how it integrates into established EDA flows, and what trust level is required before autonomous agents run in production.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: Trust and integration, not capability, are now the gating factors for agentic-EDA adoption—the metric to watch for the sector's inflection.
insights.quote: The more urgent questions are where AI is delivering measurable value today, how it can be integrated into established EDA flows, and what level of trust is required before autonomous agents can be deployed in production design.
insights.locator: Making the AI wave at DAC 2026

insights.claim: ChipAgents' multi-agent platform automates design, verification and debugging tasks while integrating with existing EDA tools rather than replacing them.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: Integration-with-incumbents positioning lowers adoption friction; a go-to-market pattern for AI-EDA startups versus rip-and-replace.
insights.locator: Experience the Future of Chip Design at ChipAgents Booth

insights.claim: On end-to-end root cause analysis using code and waveform data across commercial-scale IPs including bus fabrics, RISC-V cores, PCIe and DDR, ChipAgents achieved over 3x higher pass-at-one accuracy than state-of-the-art generic AI agents.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: A 3x accuracy edge on debug—the most time-consuming ASIC task—is the kind of quantified moat that could drive domain-specific AI-EDA adoption over generic LLM agents.
insights.quote: ChipAgents achieved over 3× higher pass-at-one accuracy than state-of-the-art generic AI agents
insights.locator: #1 Autonomous Root Cause Analysis at Commercial Scale

insights.claim: In a joint paper with STMicroelectronics, ChipAgents reduced specification reading time by 15x, formal assertion generation by 240x, and UVM environment creation by 400x, while keeping the verification engineer in full control.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: Order-of-magnitude verification speedups with a named partner (STMicro) lend credibility; if reproducible, this compresses design cycles and headcount needs.
insights.quote: specification reading time was reduced by 15×, formal assertion generation by 240×, and UVM environment creation by 400×
insights.locator: #3 The New Era of Digital Verification

insights.claim: ChipAgents argues effective AI timing closure requires domain-specific capabilities beyond general code generation—timing report understanding, RTL mapping, design-intent preservation and PPA-aware decision-making.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: Reinforces the thesis that generic LLMs are insufficient for EDA; domain specialization is the differentiator and barrier to entry.

insights.claim: ChipAgents CEO William Wang will join Analog Devices Senior Director Nimay Shah at DAC 2026 to discuss agentic AI across architectural exploration, RTL quality analysis, verification acceleration, physical design and design closure.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: Analog Devices engagement signals blue-chip customer interest in agentic EDA—an adoption signal beyond vendor marketing.
insights.locator: Catch the TechTalk on Where Agentic AI Delivers Real Value

insights.claim: DAC comes to Long Beach for the first time in 2026 with AI expected to be a central topic across the program and exhibition floor, and ChipAgents took the largest booth on the show floor.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: AI dominance at DAC and a startup taking the largest booth signals capital and mindshare shifting toward agentic EDA.
insights.locator: Making the AI wave at DAC 2026
